EA SPORTS FC 26 is a football simulation title focused on replicating modern professional football through updated squads, tactical systems, and refined gameplay mechanics. It continues the series’ emphasis on licensed teams, realistic presentation, and competitive online play, particularly in modes like Ultimate Team, Career Mode, and Clubs.  It is primarily experienced as a live-service adjacent sports platform. Most engagement is driven by seasonal content resets, roster updates, and ongoing online competition rather than a fixed narrative or standalone progression. The game’s lifecycle is defined by annual iteration, meaning players typically migrate forward each release cycle rather than staying within a single version long-term.  Its strengths are authentic presentation, strong licensing coverage, and responsive core gameplay that supports both casual and competitive play. However, it is often criticized for incremental yearly changes, heavy monetization in Ultimate Team, and a gameplay loop that can feel familiar across iterations.  Overall, EA SPORTS FC 26 functions as a competitive football simulation platform rather than a traditional game with long-term narrative depth. It is best suited for players invested in online competition, squad building, and yearly sports progression.

EA Sports FC 26 is a sensational football game that delivers one of the most exciting and immersive experiences the series has offered in years. The gameplay feels smoother and more responsive, with improved dribbling, smarter AI positioning, and more realistic player movement that make every match feel dynamic and authentic. EA also introduced separate Competitive and Authentic gameplay styles, allowing players to choose between fast-paced online action and a more realistic football simulation experience.  One of FC 26's biggest strengths is its attention to detail. The atmosphere inside stadiums is outstanding, player animations look more lifelike than ever, and the presentation closely resembles a real football broadcast. Career Mode has received meaningful improvements with new challenges and player progression systems, adding depth and replay value for long-term players.  While some players continue to criticize aspects of Ultimate Team and online gameplay, many reviewers consider FC 26 a significant step forward for the franchise thanks to its gameplay refinements, improved realism, and expanded customization options.  Overall, EA Sports FC 26 is a sensational football game that successfully balances realism, excitement, and innovation. Whether you're a casual fan looking for fun matches with friends or a dedicated football enthusiast seeking a deeper simulation experience, FC 26 offers countless hours of entertainment and stands as one of the strongest entries in the EA Sports FC series.

Great Game, But Definitively Needs Some Bug Fixes!  Overall Rating: Recommended (with caveats)    I am really enjoying EA Sports FC 26! The game is genuinely fun and has some great, funny moments that make it highly entertaining to play. However, the developers seriously need to roll out some patches because there are two very frustrating bugs ruining the experience right now.    ⚠️ Major Issues that Need Fixing:  The Controller Disconnect Bug (Player Profile Issue):  If you leave your controller idle for about 10 to 20 minutes, it automatically disconnects. The real problem is that when you turn it back on, the game refuses to recognize it as the first/primary profile. Instead, it assigns it to a second profile, forcing you to restart the game just to get your main controller working again.    The Career Mode Second-Half Camera Bug:  Whether playing Manager Career or Player Career, the first half of the match plays perfectly. However, right after the halftime menu when the second half starts, the camera completely breaks. It either gets stuck too low or glitches out completely underneath the stadium, making the game completely unplayable until you restart or reload.    Final Verdict:  It is a good and funny game at its core, but these bugs are incredibly annoying. EA, please fix the controller recognition and the Career Mode camera glitch!

I’ve always had a love-hate relationship with the FIFA and FC games over the years. But this year, EA did something that completely transcended video games. They did something that made me break down and cry for an hour.  Instead of listing the names of the Iranian National Team players, who largely stand as supporters of a cruel regime, EA chose to honor the brave Iranian protesters who were murdered by that very regime. They put their names into the game.  For years, we've criticized EA for monetization and mechanics. But with this single, courageous choice, they used their massive global platform to speak for the voiceless. They ensured that the heroes who died fighting for basic human freedom are remembered, not forgotten.  EA Team, if anyone from your studio ever reads this: Thank you from the bottom of my heart. You didn't just update a roster; you showed humanity. You have earned my respect and my love in full. I wish you nothing but the absolute best, always.

Without a doubt, FC 26 is the most stressful gaming experience I have ever had. Every single element of this game seems deliberately designed to frustrate the player, from the over-the-top celebrations to the moment-to-moment gameplay itself.  Defensively, the game is simply broken. No matter how well you position and control your players, stopping attacks feels virtually impossible. The game punishes good defending and rewards chaos.  The worst offender, however, is the rubber-band AI — what the community calls "handicap." The moment you build a comfortable lead, the game quietly tilts the odds against you. Your passes start going astray, your opponents suddenly become unstoppable, and the match mysteriously "rebalances" itself. It's not bad luck. It's the system working against you on purpose.  In summary: I hate this game. After countless sessions leaving me more stressed than entertained, I'm walking away for good. FC 26 doesn't deserve your time or your mental health.

★☆☆☆☆  I am extremely disappointed with EA Sports FC 26.  The biggest issue is that every time I try to launch the game, I'm immediately greeted with a DLC advertisement on the startup page. Instead of being able to jump into a game that I already paid for, I'm being pushed toward additional content before I can even play.  What's worse is that FC 26 won't properly start for me. The game either gets stuck, fails to launch, or refuses to get past the startup process. Many players have reported launch and startup issues with FC 26, making the experience frustrating before you even reach the main menu.  The most frustrating part is the refund situation. I can no longer get a refund because I already claimed the content associated with my purchase. Now I'm stuck with a game that I can't play and can't return.  A game should work before developers start advertising extra content. Being locked out of playing while being shown DLC promotions is a terrible customer experience.  I expected a finished product, but instead I got launch issues, unwanted DLC advertisements, and no way to get my money back. I cannot recommend EA Sports FC 26 in its current state.

I really don't understand EA. How can a company this big release a game in such a terrible state?  And honestly, I don't even care anymore that the game is pay-to-win, or that EA keeps ruining the things that were actually good about it. But if you're going to release a game on multiple platforms, at least optimize it properly. Players shouldn't have to spend days trying to fix FPS issues just to make the game run smoothly.  In other EA games, cross-platform play doesn't come with connection problems anywhere near this bad. I even checked my connection score on EA's website, and it's 93/100. Yet somehow crossplay seems to make everything even worse.  You can disable crossplay in the settings, but then you can't even play online. So what's the point of having that option in the first place?  I also looked into the "unstable connection" issue and found that there are only a handful of internet providers around the world that don't seem to have problems with it, Vodafone being one of them. So what about everyone else? Are the rest of us just supposed to deal with it?  When is EA finally going to start listening to the people who actually play their games? When is EA going to take criticism seriously and fix these problems instead of ignoring them?  If you're reading this, please share it with as many people as possible. Maybe then it'll finally reach EA.  Every year EA releases an insanely expensive game that's basically the same as the previous one, yet somehow it gets worse and worse every year.

100+ hours of gameplay. To begin with, I must emphasize that my review comes strictly from the perspective of a Manager Career and Player Career player. I have never accessed, nor do I ever intend to access, the Ultimate Team mode. I purchased the latest edition, FC 26, largely based on the promises made regarding improvements to Career Mode. From day one, I noticed a glaring bug: the game calls an offside on passes between two players who have already past the opponent's last defender (I deliberately didn't report this bug just to see how long it would take them to fix it—and it still hasn't been patched to this day). The AI implemented in this version is extremely limited and completely lacks consistency and realism. They didn't bother at all to give the AI basic logic based on real-world player or manager data, leading to transfers that make absolutely no sense. Inexperienced, low-quality players demand to be starters, and in-game events are extremely rare and meaningless. The transfer system is highly impractical, and basic features are missing—like the fact that opposing team players should already be scouted prior to a direct match. Youth academies are completely OP and unrealistic; getting players with 90+ potential is trivial and way too easy. The transition from the youth squad to the first team is too effortless, and their contract demands are utterly unrealistic.  Despite all of this, the biggest disappointment remains the Simulation (Authentic) mode. While the highest difficulty in Competitive mode poses no challenge at all and is extremely easy to beat (a real issue in itself), the situation is the exact opposite in Simulation mode. On Professional difficulty, you win 6-0 in 90% of cases, but the moment you switch to World Class, your chances of winning drop to under 5%. Possession becomes absolute hell; opposing defenders endlessly pass the ball around without giving you any chance to intercept, leaving you to finish the match with barely 25% possession most of the time. The scripting is extremely obvious! I don't understand the point of scripting in a single-player career mode at all.  5/10.

This game wants to frustrate you, it wants to anger you so you get so fed up that the other player has better cards than you that you just suck it up and buy points to open packs with. That is what this game is for, to make you pay more. If you like just feeling awful, and spending money, that's the whole game, this one is for you. The only reason I'm writing this review at all, is because I hope someone reads it and realizes it isn't worth it this year, or next year, or any year, until they fix this game and allow you to just play without getting cheated by the people who make it.

The gameplay is slow and feels sluggish, even on competitive. The AI is way too op. They time the tackles perfectly with no error ever and it's frustrating. A relegation team's goalkeeper suddenly becomes prime Neuer, but my top goalkeeper suddenly becomes a league 2 goalkeeper. How does that make sense?  The Player Career mode has also been changed, but it's still the same crap. The thing I enjoy is the Archetype feature. But other than that, it's not good. The new attribute system takes way too long to max out. I understand that it's more realistic, but I do not want to go through 10 seasons while playing every single match just to max out. They should add a setting makes you gain faster exp, just as in Manager Career mode where you can choose if your players develop faster etc.  I am in no way good at the game, and I've never said I was. People may say that it's a skill issue and it probably is, but I don't want to tryhard the game as I said. The reason I wrote this review was only because I lost an important match and wanted to get my rage out somehow..

why EA Sports FC 26 Is One of the Most Frustrating Football Games Ever Made    EA Sports FC 26 was supposed to be the game that finally redeemed the franchise after years of criticism. EA promised better gameplay, improved realism, smarter AI, and a more rewarding experience for players. Instead, many fans feel like they got the exact opposite: a broken, scripted, microtransaction-heavy mess that manages to be both exhausting and repetitive at the same time.    One of the biggest problems with FC 26 is the gameplay itself. Passing feels inconsistent, defenders randomly stop tracking runs, and player switching can feel completely broken during crucial moments. Many players online have complained that simple passes go in the wrong direction while defenders react slower than attackers no matter how highly rated they are. The result is a game where matches often feel determined more by glitches, animations, and luck than actual skill.    The so-called “meta” makes things even worse. Instead of rewarding smart football, FC 26 often rewards unrealistic gameplay tactics like constant spinning, kickoff glitches, and abuse of overpowered mechanics. Players who actually want to build attacks realistically are punished, while people abusing broken mechanics dominate online modes. For a game marketed as a football simulation, it frequently feels more like an arcade exploit competition.    Ultimate Team is another major reason people hate the game. EA continues to push aggressive monetization through endless promos, expensive packs, and constant Squad Building Challenges. Many players feel the game is designed less around football and more around encouraging people to spend money. Some frustrated fans have even described the mode as “a casino game” rather than a sports game. The grind becomes exhausting because no matter how much time players invest, EA constantly releases stronger cards that make previous progress feel pointless.    Technical issues only add fuel to the fire. PC players have reported severe input delay, anti-cheat problems, crashes, and lag that make online matches nearly unplayable. Even after patches, many users say the game still feels sluggish and inconsistent. Nothing destroys a competitive game faster than feeling like your controls are fighting against you.    What frustrates longtime fans most is that this cycle keeps repeating every year. EA markets each new release as revolutionary, but many players believe FC 26 is just another recycled version of previous games with slightly updated graphics and new monetization tactics. Community backlash across forums, Reddit, and social media has been intense, with some fans calling it the worst football game EA has ever made. Even players who initially enjoyed the game eventually admitted that the frustration builds over time as gameplay flaws become impossible to ignore.    To be fair, FC 26 is not completely terrible. Some reviewers praised its visuals, presentation, and improvements to offline modes. But flashy graphics and licensed stadiums cannot save a football game when the gameplay itself feels broken. At its core, players want a game that is fair, responsive, and rewarding. Too often, FC 26 feels scripted, unbalanced, and designed more around engagement metrics than genuine fun.

"Trying to get objective rewards feels like a chore and a punishment rather than a fun challenge. There is no transparent rank check, no fair matchmaking, and no protection for casual players, mostly in Live Events. The matchmaking in Live Events (like World Tour Silver Superstars) is an absolute nightmare. The system feels completely random and has zero skill-based balancing. As an average player, I am constantly forced to play against much higher skilled players. You can easily go on a 20-game losing streak just trying to complete a simple objective. EA forces you to play these modes if you want to earn objective rewards, but they turn it into a miserable experience by pairing casual players with hardcore, high-tier opponents. EA needs to fix this, because right now, it's completely trash."

Look at EA's forums and you'll see how prevalent this is. Do not buy this game if you want a fair online competitive experience.

Another installment of an awful game.    First, the positives: this year, the game doesn’t feel like it has any completely broken mechanics. Most mechanics seem relatively balanced, and it’s rare to see players consistently exploiting one specific mechanic nowadays.    Negatives  Game Modes    The game feels like it only has two legitimate game modes: Clubs and Ultimate Team. If you plan on playing Career Mode, you’ll quickly become disgusted by how unrealistic and repetitive it gets.    Problems with Clubs    This year, they implemented a slower progression system, preventing players from reaching max level within the first week. That is objectively a good change.    Other additions are also positive, such as allowing players to join up to three different clubs and the ability to enter matches as a substitute. Although the substitute feature is glitchy and can sometimes freeze or crash your game, the idea itself is very good.    However, they removed the ability to message someone directly in your lobby. Sometimes you play with a random teammate who doesn’t have a microphone, and now you basically have to add them as a friend just to send actual messages instead of the default preset nonsense.    Another issue is the AI players in Clubs. It would make sense for them to gradually improve throughout the year. They don’t need to become elite-level players, but at the time of writing this review, the AI is genuinely terrible. Even top players would struggle to defend simple plays because of how bad the AI defenders are.    Regarding Ultimate Team    Ultimate Team is fun at first, but it quickly becomes overly repetitive. The fact that a new promotion drops every single week completely ruins the sense of progression.    Saving coins for a great player doesn’t feel rewarding anymore. You grind endlessly, finally get the player you wanted, and two weeks later their price has dropped by 90%. That’s not healthy for the mode, and it only encourages people to spend real money just to keep up with the constant cycle of content — especially if you actually have a life outside the game, unlike me.    For competitive players, it would also be nice to have proper rankings showing the percentage of players in each Rivals division and FUT Champions rank.    Gameplay    Going back to the positives, I mentioned earlier that no mechanic feels overwhelmingly broken this year. Unfortunately, that’s about the only genuinely positive thing I can say about the gameplay.    The gameplay is wildly inconsistent. Passing — even with players who have excellent passing stats — feels like flipping a coin. You either get a perfect pass or some ridiculous animation that sends the ball straight to the defender.    Dribbling feels unresponsive, and defenders controlled by AI are often better than actual players. Your opponent can simply let the AI defend for them, and it will still do an excellent job. That’s terrible game design.    Crosses and long shots are also almost useless. Crosses are usually floated straight into the goalkeeper’s hands, and outside-the-box shots end up flying into the corner flag 90% of the time if you’re lucky.    Goalkeepers are undeniably better than last year, but they still concede absurd near-post goals far too often.    The tactics system also needs a complete rework. It forces you into specific formations, and the role changes often have very limited impact on gameplay.    Defensive line settings feel completely unrealistic. A “high line” barely pushes beyond midfield, and the moment the opponent attacks, defenders immediately sprint backwards instead of maintaining shape. A low line is even worse — the defenders practically sit inside the goal. Watch an actual football match, developers.    Features that used to exist, like manually adjusting player movement in attack and defense, are gone. On top of that, experimenting with different tactics is basically punished if you want to stay competitive. The “meta” dominates everything, as if we’re playing Call of Duty instead of a football game.    Final Thoughts    To conclude: this is another horrible game from a horrible franchise that has only survived because it has no real competition. Year after year, they continue to deliver mediocre products, and people keep buying them anyway.

EA once again manages to hit a new low. I don’t even think I need to explain what a pay-to-win system is anymore, because this game is basically the definition of it.  Even if you spend a stupid amount of money to build a top-tier squad, you still won’t beat a player whose skill level is around the same as yours if he simply has a better team. Squad quality matters way too much, and the gameplay balance is complete garbage.  The in-game economy is absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. The developers are genuinely ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ shameless. This year there’s an insane amount of packs, and the funniest part is that they don’t even show the real drop rates. They just write “<1%”. Okay, but what is the ACTUAL chance? 0.9%? 0.01%? 0.0001%? They deliberately hide the numbers, which honestly feels like straight-up scamming.  And the AI in this game is completely ♥♥♥♥♥♥. I used to enjoy Career Mode on high difficulties, but in this game it’s unbearable. A perfect example is Squad Battles in Ultimate Team. On higher difficulties it’s practically unplayable because the AI is blatantly scripted. The bot plays like it’s reading your inputs. And the funniest ♥♥♥♥ is that even if you have a squad worth 5 million coins like mine, you still won’t win consistently — I guarantee it.  Why? Because it’s obviously designed to stop players from farming too many points so they get worse rewards at the end of the week. And those rewards are usually complete trash anyway, meaning you’ll never pack a good player unless you get insanely lucky. The entire AI system feels intentionally designed to frustrate people into rage-buying packs after losses.  I could honestly list at least 10 more problems with this game, but here’s the conclusion:  Players, I seriously encourage you to boycott EA and every future game in this franchise. EA already strangled Konami by buying up football licenses and basically killed PES — a game that was genuinely more enjoyable to play and where, at least years ago, the developers actually seemed to give a ♥♥♥♥ about players.  Never buy EA games. I genuinely hope this review convinces at least someone not to waste money on products from this company.  And by the way, almost nobody reads the EULA when buying games. You don’t even truly own the digital product — you’re just paying for permission to use it.
